cultures("Hakili"; "Manika'i"; "Kukani"; "Wainani").

% For each island, match it to exactly one year and one culture.
{match(I, Y, C) : years(Y), cultures(C)} = 1 :- islands(I).

% No option in any category will ever be used more than once.
:- match(I1, Y1, C1), match(I2, Y2, C2), I1 != I2, Y1 == Y2.
:- match(I1, Y1, C1), match(I2, Y2, C2), I1 != I2, C1 == C2.
:- match(I1, Y1, C1), match(I2, Y2, C2), Y1 != Y2, C1 == C2.

% 1. Jujihm was discovered in 1768.
:- match(I, Y, C), I = "Jujihm", Y != 1768.

% 2. Verinya was discovered in 1761.
:- match(I, Y, C), I = "Verinya", Y != 1761.

% 3. Jujihm was discovered 14 years after the island on which the Wainani people lived.
% So the Wainani island was discovered in 1768 - 14 = 1754.
:- match(I, Y, C), C = "Wainani", Y != 1754.

% Ensure the Wainani island is discovered 14 years before Jujihm (1768)
% So Wainani's year must be 1754, and Jujihm's year is 1768
% We already enforce Jujihm = 1768, so we only need to ensure Wainani = 1754
% But we already have that in the above constraint.

% 4. The island on which the Kukani people lived was discovered 7 years after Fushil.
% So Kukani's year = Fushil's year + 7
:- match("Fushil", Y_f, _), match(I, Y_k, "Kukani"), Y_k != Y_f + 7.

% 5.1 The island discovered in 1768 and Fushil are different.
:- match(I, Y, C), Y = 1768, I = "Fushil".

% 5.2 The island discovered in 1768 is the island on which the Hakili people lived or Fushil.
% But 5.1 says they are different, so it must be Hakili.
% So the island discovered in 1768 must be Hakili.
% Therefore, if year=1768, then culture must be Hakili
:- match(I, Y, C), Y = 1768, C != "Hakili".

% 5.3 Fushil is the island on which the Hakili people lived or the island discovered in 1768.
% So either Fushil has culture Hakili, or Fushil was discovered in 1768.
% But 5.1 says Fushil ≠ island discovered in 1768, so Fushil must have culture Hakili.
:- match(I, Y, C), I = "Fushil", C != "Hakili", Y != 1768.
